message:
fix: validate SMTP port and prevent silent default-revert (#8894)

The SMTP port input previously coerced its value to a number on every
keystroke and fell back to 587 whenever the field was empty or NaN, so
clearing the field silently snapped it back to the default with no
indication that the user's value was being discarded.

Track the raw string locally, validate as integer in [1, 65535], surface
inline error (role="alert" + aria-describedby), and disable the Test
button while invalid. Only push valid integers into the shared config.

Fixes #8868

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -401,7 +401,8 @@
         "testNotification": "Test Email Notification",
         "configureFirst": "Please configure all required email fields first",
         "testSuccess": "Test email sent successfully!",
-        "testFailed": "Failed to send test email"
+        "testFailed": "Failed to send test email",
+        "invalidSmtpPort": "SMTP port must be a number between 1 and 65535."
       },
       "tip": "Configure notification channels here, then add them to specific alert rules in the Alert Rules editor. Each rule can have multiple notification channels with different configurations.",
       "browser": {
